reason: Response A shows more sophisticated craft: vivid concrete images ("no one has ever held a moment in their hands"), self-referential moves ("even as you read that word, it has already slipped away"), varied sentence rhythm, and a memorable closing turn, all hallmarks of the more capable Sonnet tier. Response B is competent but flatter and more formulaic, relying on generic abstractions and conventional self-help phrasing ("productivity systems," "authenticity," "the medium through which we live and love"), which reads more like the smaller Haiku model.
